_comment: Pattern-match lookup mapping a snapshot stat-line to the text written for accessory & bracelet effect lines. KEYS: use "<index>:<type>" when a stat's meaning depends on its type (statLineText.js prefers this), else "<index>" alone for stats whose meaning is consistent across types - e.g. index 0 is ally-attack as a ring line (type 54 -> key "0:54") but means something different as a type-50 line (key "0:50"), while 151/16000001 are type-independent. Each entry is { text, scale?, decimals?, placeholder? }: `text` is the literal cell value, an optional `{value}` placeholder is replaced by the stat's `value` multiplied by `scale` (default 1); `decimals` fixes the number of decimal places (e.g. ring ally-damage uses 2 -> "7.50") while omitting it trims trailing zeros and rounds to at most 3 dp (e.g. bracelet -> "7.5" or "9"); and `placeholder:true` flags entries whose exact in-game/sheet string is not yet known (grep 'placeholder' to find them - replace `text` with the real string; no code change needed). Consumed by the SHEET via the statLineText.js cell transform. Covers the bracelet stat table in planning/sheetFillingPlanning.md (tiers are High>Mid>Low). The fixed base attributes (spec 16, swift 18, the main stats 3/4/5/6, and the other combat stats 15/17/19/20) are NOT lines - statLineText.js drops them (base:true on accessories, else the BASE_STATS index set) so the rolled lines pack in item order, while their values go to their own cells via braceletSpec/braceletSwift/braceletMain and necklaceMain/ear*Main/ring*Main.

ring:0:54.comment: Ring ally-attack line (index 0, type 54). The ring section uses the abbreviated wording.

bracelet:0.comment: Bracelet ally-attack line (index 0). The bracelet section uses the full wording.
